channel.title: studio on tired, since 1985
channel.description: Recent content in studio on tired, since 1985

item.description:
What’s the best way to handle a personal sample library in Logic Pro?

  • Slice and export the WAV files?
  • Tempos?
    • These were recorded sans-click wayyy before I had a DAW
  • Naming convention?

I’m only thinking about this as I’ve recently came across my old Olympus micro-cassette recorder and have recorded both sides into a Logic Pro session. There are loads of great loop points, textures and background noise (side A alone consists of TV commercial audio).

Too tired yesterday to really accomplish anything. I continued to read Ocean of Sound by David Toop and think more of what I want to achieve in terms of sound, atmosphere and mood.

I spent about an hour blending tones driven locally by an LFO to envelope.

Everything now “talks” with Logic Pro. The modular can be controlled via USB -> MIDI or via the Korg SQ-1 via CV/Gate which can be clocked via Logic Pro.

In terms of the modular, I’ve been investigating pan/fade module

At the moment I feel like I’m getting reacquainted with my gear. Reading through “Analog Synthesis” by Reinhard Schmitz I’m gaining a better foothold on the fundamentals of modular synthesis that I didn’t realise I was missing.
